'''Priapulins''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sapient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pria/Legends|Pria]], one of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Priapulin species were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Gastropod/Legends|gastropods]] resembling large worms with five knobby notochords arranged around their tubular length. When fully extended flat, they measured at four meters in length, however preferred the configuration of an upright S shape. They had three pairs of eyes, and the underside of their bodies were covered in thick bristles. The spiny appendages along the edges of their bodies were specialized for a number of purposes, being hooked, thorny, or spatulate. Used in conjunction, these appendages were used as a form of locomotion or could be used as hands and fingers for grasping tools or wielding weapons.<ref name="TEGAS" />

==Society and culture==
[[File:Priapaulin_G8.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A group of Priapulins.]]

Native to the ocean planet [[Pria/Legends|Pria]], the Priapulin maintained a civilization dedicated to peace and were noted as having avoided many conflicts for centuries. While the species dominated the food-chain on Pria, they managed to cultivate a symbiotic relationship with the other sentients on the planet, including the [[Crast]] species. In exchange for carrying and raising their offspring, the Crast would offer themselves up as food for Priapulins, considering it a great honor to be chosen for consumption.<ref name="Rogue Planet">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

Known for their strong sense of humor, Priapulins adhered to a strict code of loyalty and honor. While [[Priap]] was their native language, Priapulins spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] in a smooth whisper. Priapulins measured distance in "[[salt pan]]s" (nine of which was equal to eleven [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]) and time in "[[tidal hour]]s" (one of which equaled ten hours).<ref name="TEGAS" />

==History==
Inhabiting a planet deep within space controlled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] since its founding, the Priapulin did not seem to play an active role in influencing galactic history, and no records indicate that the planet hosted any battles during the constant threats which threatened the Republic over the millennia.<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Priapulins in the galaxy==
While not widely spotted far from their homeworld, some Priapulin found employ as [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and common laborers. One such Priapulin was [[Charza Kwinn]], a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who was often employed by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to transport [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] to [[Planet/Legends|planet]] throughout the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on missions. During the early years of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], Kwinn transported [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] as the two searched for the missing Jedi [[Vergere]].<ref name="Rogue Planet" />

[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Priapulins were known to exist, with at least one serving in the ranks of the Jedi Order long before the [[New Sith Wars]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Skwelli]] was highly proficient in the art of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] and was noted for wielding six [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] simultaneously with recordings of his prowess memorialized within ancient Jedi [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]].<ref name="The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force real-life book">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Wikipedia: Priapulida|Priapulida]] are a group of real-world worms.
